markdown:
#  When does the 'Signature Required' column in the Change Orders tool display the signer's name? 
##  Background 
When users link their DocuSign® account to a Procore project and send a change order for signature, the 'Signature Required' column in the Commitments or Prime Contracts tab shows the name of the person who needs to sign.
##  Answer 
In the Change Orders tool, the 'Signature Required' column in the Commitments or Prime Contracts tab shows the name of the person whose signature is required. This name appears after a change order is sent for signature (see [Complete a Commitment Change Order Contract with DocuSign®](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/complete-a-commitment-change-order-cco-with-docusign) or [Complete a Prime Contract Change Order with DocuSign®](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/prime-contracts-project/tutorials/complete-a-prime-contract-change-order-with-docusign)). While the signature is pending, the required signer's name is visible. Once the document is signed, the name disappears from the column to indicate no signatures are required.
## See Also
  * [What are the default columns in the Contracts tab of the Commitments tool?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-the-default-columns-in-the-contracts-tab-of-the-commitments-tool)
  * [What are the DocuSign® fields and can I customize them?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-the-docusign-fields-and-can-i-customize-them)
  * [What gets copied over to a new project from a project template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-gets-copied-over-to-a-new-project-when-applying-a-project-template)
title: When does the 'Signature Required' column in the Change Orders tool display the signer's name?
